title: Nelitris jambosella

intro: Nelitris jambosella, also known as Jambosa, is a plant species belonging to the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to tropical Asia and widely distributed in India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory properties. Nelitris jambosella is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nelitris jambosella include decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
